articles.title: Bloodbath At D-Street: Sensex Plunges Over 750 Pts; Here's Why Stock Market Is Falling
articles.description: Indian equities tumbled on July 22 as tariffs, geopolitical tensions, soaring oil prices, a weaker rupee and rising US yields rattled investors., Markets, Times Now

articles.title: Japanese Yen Hits Nearly 40-Year Low As Strong Dollar, Oil Surge Raise Intervention Risks
articles.description: The Japanese yen slipped to its weakest level since 1986 as rising oil prices, higher US Treasury yields and safe-haven demand boosted the dollar. The yen traded near 163 per dollar, raising concerns over possible intervention by Japanese authorities. Analysts expect the currency pair to remain in a higher range amid global uncertainties

articles.title: India bonds slip on oil spike, bargain hunters curb losses
articles.description: Indian government bonds weakened in early trade as oil prices extended gains. Rising crude prices could widen India's trade deficit and stoke inflationary pressure. Bargain buying and state-run bank bids limited the losses in the domestic bond market. Hopes of a peace deal and RBI foreign exchange measures aided sentiment. Traders await an update on India's inclusion in the Bloomberg Global Aggregate Index.

articles.title: SIP in a Volatile 2026 Market: Expert Strategies to Stay the Course
articles.description: New Delhi [India], July 22: Sharp market swings have defined 2026 so far. Global conflicts, high crude oil prices, heavy investor selling, and currency pressure have kept markets uncertain. The Sensex has also experienced sharp corrections in multiple trading sessions. Even so, retail investors have continued with Systematic Investment Plans (SIPs). As per recent data, SIP inflows touched a record ₹32,087 crore in March 2026.

articles.title: UK inflation falls to 2.6% thanks to lower petrol prices
articles.description: A drop in the costs of motor fuels helped UK inflation cool to 2.6 per cent in June from 2.8 per cent in May, the Office for National Statistics said.

articles.title: E20 fuel may lower mileage in older E10 vehicles by up to 5%: Govt tells Parliament
articles.description: E20 petrol: Government informs Parliament on fuel economy, engine damage, and future blending plans.

articles.title: Global Markets: Tech Earnings Loom Amid Geopolitical and Economic Challenges
articles.description: Global stock markets showed mixed movements on Wednesday. Gains were tempered by caution ahead of major tech earnings and geopolitical tensions in the Middle East affecting oil prices. Analysts focus on semiconductor recoveries and anticipate impactful earnings from tech giants Alphabet and Tesla amid global economic uncertainties.

articles.title: Trump plays down prospects of Iran talks amid Red Sea threat
articles.description: Trump downplays Iran talks amid escalating tensions and threats to Red Sea shipping, as military actions and oil prices rise.

articles.title: Oil prices rise on supply fears
articles.description: Singapore: Global oil prices were higher in early trading on Wednesday as investors reacted to renewed military escalation between the United States and Iran, raising concerns over potential disruptions to crude supplies from the Middle East.

articles.title: BPCL Q1 Results 2026 LIVE: PSU stock to report earnings today - Here's what brokerages expect from the OMC stock
articles.description: BPCL Q1 Results 2026 LIVE: Brokerage firm Kotak Institutional Equities said adjusted for LPG compensation, BPCL likely to report EBITDA loss of Rs185 bn due to higher crude costs and very high likely losses in petrol and diesel marketing. LPG under-recoveries will increase significantly in 1Q.
